name: Asian Pacific National Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving the San Gabriel Valley and Los Angeles since 1990, specializing in commercial real estate lending and relationship-based banking.
description_long:
Asian Pacific National Bank was founded in 1990 by Chairman & CEO John Hou and has established itself as a bedrock institution in the San Gabriel Valley and greater Los Angeles region. The bank operates as a Minority Depository Institution (MDI) and maintains FDIC insurance, backing all deposits with the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. Over three decades, the bank has built its reputation on deep community ties and a commitment to understanding local business needs.

The bank offers a range of consumer and commercial banking services, including certificates of deposit with competitive interest rates, commercial real estate lending programs, and relationship-based lending solutions. They emphasize flexible terms, competitive fixed and variable rates, and customized financing options designed to compete effectively against larger institutions. The bank also actively seeks referral partnerships with CPAs, brokers, and business consultants, indicating a B2B-focused growth strategy.

Asian Pacific National Bank distinguishes itself through its high-touch, relationship-based service model rather than transactional banking. They employ experienced relationship bankers to guide clients through lending processes and provide personalized service. The bank's 5-star rating from Bauer Financial signals strong financial health and institutional stability. Their specialization in commercial real estate reflects expertise tailored to local market conditions and client needs in Southern California.

The main caveat is that as a regional community bank, Asian Pacific National Bank likely lacks the full suite of products and digital conveniences offered by national banks. The website content focuses heavily on commercial real estate and business lending, suggesting consumer banking services may be secondary offerings. Geographic service area appears limited to Southern California based on their positioning, which may restrict accessibility for customers outside the region. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of deposit (CDs) with competitive interest rates
Commercial real estate lending and specialized lending programs
Flexible loan terms customized to client needs
Relationship banking with dedicated relationship bankers
Multiple branch locations across service area
Referral partnership programs for financial professionals
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Bauer Financial 5-star rating indicating superior financial stability
Specialized commercial real estate lending programs with flexible, streamlined processes
Competitive CD rates described as higher than other deposit accounts
Relationship-based banking model with experienced relationship bankers for personalized guidance
30+ years of local market expertise and community presence in San Gabriel Valley and LA
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to San Gabriel Valley and Los Angeles region
Website provides minimal information about consumer checking/savings products and rates
No mention of digital banking platforms, mobile apps, or online account opening capabilities
Heavy commercial real estate focus may indicate consumer banking services are not primary focus
Limited details on account types, fee structures, or minimum deposit requirements

contact.address: 333 W Valley Blvd, San Gabriel, CA 91776

best_for: Small to mid-sized businesses seeking commercial real estate financing in Southern California
Local entrepreneurs and business owners in the San Gabriel Valley and Los Angeles region
High-net-worth individuals and families preferring relationship-based banking over large institutions
CPAs, brokers, and consultants seeking referral partnership opportunities
diagnosis: Asian Pacific National Bank is best suited for Southern California-based business owners and entrepreneurs seeking specialized commercial real estate financing with personalized service and local market expertise. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to the San Gabriel Valley and Los Angeles region, combined with limited publicly available information about consumer banking products and digital banking capabilities, suggesting this institution prioritizes commercial over retail banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 333 W Valley Blvd, San Gabriel, CA 91776
